principle: A change to the served verify path is byte-exact (greedy-identical) IFF it preserves the order of every floating-point reduction. byte-exact <=> reduction-order-preserving. Every material bandwidth-saving lever recovers BW by REASSOCIATING a reduction, therefore is greedy-unsafe. PPL-pass != greedy-identical: a reassociating change can be PPL-neutral (teacher-forced) yet identity-breaking (free-running argmax cascade).

roofline_perfect_retile_ceiling.label: Roofline perfect-f->1 re-tile ceiling on the int4-GEMM (greedy-UNSAFE)

definition.preserving: Does not change the order in which the FP reduction accumulates (pipeline/scheduling knob, or no exposed knob -> stays at deployed order). Byte-exact / greedy-identical.
definition.reassociating: Re-partitions or re-combines an FP reduction -> different float rounding order -> can flip a low-bit argmax -> greedy-unsafe.

levers.lever: tile/kernel-config (no exposed num_splits/max_par knob -> deployed in-kernel order)
levers.result: +0.00 byte-exact-safe; Marlin is the UNIQUE sm_86 int4 GEMM

levers.lever: split-K / BLOCK_K / num_warps re-tile (re-partitions K reduction)
levers.result: greedy-UNSAFE (realistic_splitk_greedy_safe=false); the ~16% BW slack, +12.6..+31.4 TPS unsafe
breaks byte-exactness on 3/4 shapes; identity-breaking UB +0.64 < +2

kernels.kernel: Triton 3D split-KV attention (head-256 sliding x35 + head-512 global x7)
kernels.reduction: online-softmax segment merge (reduce_segments: max + exp-rescale + sum-PV) across the KV-partition axis

levers.result: +0.2613 byte-exact (kernel +6.11% on the tunable slice); ref #428 bit-identical supply ceiling

levers.lever: BLOCK_M / BLOCK_Q tile (BLOCK_Q 4->1 ⇒ grid 96->288 CTAs; re-partitions the split-KV reduction)
levers.result: greedy-UNSAFE: census 53.1% identical (byte_exact=0, 30/64 diverge), floor-proven REAL (cy0ijlit 100%); wall -5.65 TPS evaporates (gyw2ksvs) -- LEVER #21

kernels.reduction: hidden->vocab projection K-accumulation (produces logits; argmax selects token)

levers.lever: full-hidden read (deployed order; verify GEMM reads full 12288 hidden)
levers.result: pinned; candidate-restricted read 2.1x SLOWER + correctness-impossible -> no byte-exact lever, no BW headroom
would reorder -> greedy-unsafe, but 0.64% of verify -> ~0 BW headroom

kernels.reduction: sum-of-squares over hidden (input/post-attn/pre&post-feedforward norms, Gemma q/k norms, final norm)

levers.result: pinned; memory-trivial at bs=1, not BW-bound -> no headroom either way

kernels.kernel: drafter fused-sparse-argmax (not verify; listed for completeness)

levers.result: +0.00 (served default wins the full 45-config grid sub-us)

reduction_enumeration.completeness_claim: Every FP reduction in the served Gemma-4-E4B M=8 verify forward (+ lm_head) falls into one of these 4 families. Each is either (a) pinned to deployed order = byte-exact with NO material BW headroom, or (b) reassociable-for-BW-but-greedy-unsafe. No reduction is in class (a) with material BW headroom; no member of class (b) is greedy-safe.

families.name: GEMM K-reduction (W4A16 contraction accumulate)
families.order: pinned (Marlin in-kernel split-K = f(M); no Python knob)

families.name: Attention split-KV online-softmax merge (reduce_segments)
families.where: Triton 3D: head-256 sliding x35 + head-512 global x7
families.bw_note: byte-exact num_stages -> +0.26 only; material BW only via partition change (reassociating); FLAG-2 surface possibly larger

ppl_trap.why: PPL is teacher-forced (conditions on ground-truth prefix; robust to sub-ULP per-logit perturbation). Greedy identity is free-running (conditions on the model's own argmax; a single flip cascades).
ppl_trap.lesson: Certify greedy identity by a served same-path census, never by PPL or an a-priori byte-exact assertion. #442's a-priori assertion was wrong; only the census caught it.

flag2_triton_attn_surface.upper_bound_note: if all sliding (x35) + global (x7) route through Triton 3D split-KV in verify
flag2_triton_attn_surface.robustness_argument: Even at the 14.19% upper bound: (1) the byte-exact knob (num_stages) is partition-invariant -> ceiling stays #447 +0.26 (and #442's served wall A/B on the full head-256+512 surface realized -5.65: the 3D path is occupancy-saturated past 80 SMs, retiling adds CTA overhead, not speedup; deleting the whole kernel caps at +4.27). (2) The material BW of the larger surface is recoverable only by a partition change (BLOCK_M/BLOCK_Q/num_splits) = greedy-unsafe (#442 identity 0.531). The correction only enlarges the greedy-unsafe column; it does not move headroom into the byte-exact column. closed_lever_count stays 21.

new_lever_21.lever: Triton 3D split-KV attention tile re-tile (BLOCK_M/BLOCK_Q) -- the reassociating sibling of #447 num_stages
new_lever_21.mechanism: Re-tile the Triton 3D split-KV attention partition (BLOCK_M 16->4, BLOCK_Q 4->1) to cash the attention BW (modeled isolated +15.86 -> 483.0).
new_lever_21.closing_number_text: greedy-UNSAFE (census 53.1% identical, floor-proven REAL) AND wall -5.65 TPS (evaporates, 475.88 < 481.53)
new_lever_21.reason: The BLOCK_M/BLOCK_Q partition change re-partitions the 3D split-KV online-softmax reduction -> FP-reassociation -> flipped argmax -> greedy-UNSAFE. Served greedy census = 34/64 = 53.1% identical (byte_exact=0, 30 prompts diverge, first flip @ tok 290), proven REAL not eager-FP by a 100% default-vs-default floor (cy0ijlit). The injector's a-priori byte-exact argument was EMPIRICALLY REFUTED -- #450's split-K reassociation hazard lives in the Triton attention too, not just the Marlin GEMM. Additionally wall-negative: -5.65 TPS (acceptance-drift e_accept 3.8416->3.8214 + grid 96->288 CTA overhead on the occupancy-saturated 3D path). 4th kernel-tiling strict-NULL (#447/#448/#449/#442); 5th isolated-op collapse (+15.86 isolated -> -5.65 served). PPL passed (2.3767) but byte_exact_and_ppl_pass=False -- PPL-pass != greedy-identical. num_stages-only sibling stays byte-exact +0.26 (#447).
